prompt: portrait of extreme close-up of a person confronting their reflection in a rainy window at night, three-quarter view facing right, positioned in left third of frame, right side of frame empty and dark, warm light on face, cold blue fill from behind. a moment of impact frozen in time — something is about to break, fall, or vanish. The subject is caught in the last second before change. The scene is inner state shown through posture and expression — a person pressing their forehead against cold glasss, a reflection in a rain-streaked window that distorts the faces, or a a long narrow corridor receding into darkness behind the subject the subject. THE CENTRAL VISUAL: the subject stands at a window, face calm and composed — but their reflection in the glass shows them with furrowed brow and mouth open as if shouting. The reflection and the person are clearly the same figure, but the expressions are opposite. This is the first thing the viewer sees. Everything else in the frame exists to support this detail.. macro lens, f/1.8 shallow depth of field, subject fills frame, intimate detail, pores and textures visible. Both the subject and their reflection are in the frame with equal visual weight. The contradiction between them is the composition — the image is split between reality and reflection.. Visual tension: imminent loss — the viewer sees something about to disappear and feels urgency. Key visual detail: motion blur on one element while everything else is frozen, or cracks spreading through a solid surface. Fracture lines or a face half-lit, half in deep shadow showing something hidden inside. warm orange key light on subject, cold blue fill light, cinematic color contrast, extremely blurred background bokeh, out-of-focus city lights, very dark, shallow depth of field f/1.4, contemplative, tension, duality, thoughtful, cinematic portrait. intimate portraiture, natural window light, shallow depth of field, slight underexposure, muted color palette. skin texture, fabric wrinkles, condensation on glass, worn wood, old paper, tarnished metal. Shot like intimate portrait photography. All emotion comes from the human body — face, hands, posture — never from visual effects. Metaphor comes from the physical environment (architecture, light, weather), not from overlays or digital manipulation. The image must look like it was taken with a real camera.. 4:5 aspect ratio, no text or writing in the image, single subject focus
